lesson: In subset backtracking, append path[:] (a copy) to results before recursing, since the shared list mutates via later pop().
Skip duplicate permutations by sorting first, then at each slot skip nums[i]==nums[i-1] unless the earlier equal value is already used.
task: permutations of a multiset without duplicates

lesson: Sort candidates so a candidate exceeding remaining lets you `break` (not `continue`) to prune the whole remaining branch in one step.
Track column and both diagonals (row-col, row+col) as three sets for O(1) conflict checks instead of rescanning the board each placement.
Mark a visited grid cell in place with a sentinel char and restore it after recursion instead of allocating a separate visited-set per DFS path.
task: word search in a 2D letter grid

lesson: Precompute row/col/3x3-box used-digit sets once, then maintain them incrementally during backtracking instead of rescanning the board per candidate check.
task: sudoku solver with row/col/box candidate sets

lesson: When digit d maps to bit d (1-indexed), extract candidates via `low = cand & -cand; d = low.bit_length()-1; cand &= cand-1` — do not shift cand while incrementing d separately, that desyncs the digit-to-bit mapping.
task: hard sudoku solver with bitmask MRV heuristic

lesson: Represent occupied columns/diagonals as integer bitmasks and shift diag1<<1/diag2>>1 per row descent so each queen placement is O(1) instead of O(n) set lookups.
task: N-Queens solution counting via bitmask DFS
